How do I restrict a Google Cloud API key to my Android app using the package name and SHA-1 fingerprint in a React Native Expo project?
output:
import React from 'react';
import { View, Text, Linking, Button } from 'react-native';

const GOOGLE_CLOUD_CREDENTIALS_URL = 'https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials';
const PLAY_CONSOLE_URL = 'https://play.google.com/console';

export default function RestrictApiKeyInstructions() {
  return (
    <View style={{ flex: 1, padding: 24, justifyContent: 'center' }}>
      <Text style={{ fontSize: 18, fontWeight: 'bold', marginBottom: 12 }}>
        Restrict Google Cloud API Key to Your Android App
      </Text>
      <Text style={{ marginBottom: 8 }}>
        1. Go to Google Cloud Credential Manager:
      </Text>
      <Button title="Open Google Cloud Credentials" onPress={() => Linking.openURL(GOOGLE_CLOUD_CREDENTIALS_URL)} />
      <Text style={{ marginVertical: 8 }}>
        2. Click "Create Credentials" > "API Key".
      </Text>
      <Text style={{ marginBottom: 8 }}>
        3. In the modal, click "Edit API key".
      </Text>
      <Text style={{ marginBottom: 8 }}>
        4. Under "Key restrictions" > "Application restrictions", choose "Android apps".
      </Text>
      <Text style={{ marginBottom: 8 }}>
        5. Click "Add an item" and enter your android package name (e.g. com.company.myapp) and the SHA-1 certificate fingerprint.
      </Text>
      <Text style={{ marginBottom: 8 }}>
        6. Click "Done" and then "Save".
      </Text>
      <Text style={{ marginTop: 16, fontWeight: 'bold' }}>Tip:</Text>
      <Text style={{ marginBottom: 8 }}>
        You can find your SHA-1 fingerprint in the Google Play Console or your Expo project dashboard.
      </Text>
      <Button title="Open Google Play Console" onPress={() => Linking.openURL(PLAY_CONSOLE_URL)} />
    </View>
  );
}
